context_analysis.meeting_objective: Northwind AI - Lead AI Engineer - R2 Technical + System Design - Meera Krishnan
context_analysis.historical_context: Cleared recruiter screen and R1. Strong RAG/evaluation background; applying up-level from Senior to Lead.

nodes.content: Thanks Priya, happy to be here. I'm a Senior AI Engineer at BrightPath, about four years in, mostly building RAG systems and LLM-powered search and support tools.

The main one is a customer-support assistant. It's a RAG pipeline - retrieval over our docs in a vector store, then an LLM that can call a few tools like ticket lookup and order status. It handles a good chunk of tier-1 tickets, so I'd call it our multi-agent support system.

Fair - in production it's really one agent with tool calls. I prototyped a planner-and-executor split in a side project, but it never went to production. So multi-agent orchestration at scale is honestly newer territory for me.

That part I'm solid on. I built an offline eval set of a few hundred real queries with expected answers, ran LLM-as-judge for answer relevance and faithfulness, and tracked those metrics on every prompt change before shipping. It caught several regressions.

We logged requests and responses to a dashboard and had some latency and error metrics. We didn't really have formal compliance requirements, so there wasn't a structured audit trail or policy layer - I haven't worked much with things like NIST or model-risk frameworks.

Okay - I'd ingest the interview transcript and the candidate resume, chunk and embed them, then run a RAG step to pull the relevant parts against the job description. Then an LLM scores the candidate on a rubric and outputs a recommendation. I'd store results in a database and show them in a dashboard for the recruiter to review.

I'd probably have the recruiter click approve in the dashboard before anything is sent, and then call the ATS and email APIs. I haven't designed that lifecycle in a lot of detail before - I'd want to think more about how the pieces coordinate.

I'd add retries, and maybe a validation step to check the output format. If it's clearly wrong we'd fall back to a default or flag it. I'll admit I haven't formalized confidence thresholds or an escalation flow in production.

I'd add more workers behind a queue and cache the embeddings so we're not recomputing them. Beyond that - things like model tiering or backpressure - I know the terms but haven't had to tune a system at that scale myself.

I led a two-engineer squad on the support-assistant feature - I planned the work and reviewed their PRs, and coordinated with one other team on the API. I haven't owned cross-organization contracts or led a larger group yet, but stepping up to that kind of leadership is exactly why I'm interested in a lead role.

Honestly, going deeper on true multi-agent orchestration and on production governance and observability. My depth is in RAG and evaluation; the agentic-platform and compliance side is where I most want to grow.
One question from me - for someone stepping up into this lead role, what kind of ramp-up or mentorship would there be on the multi-agent and platform side?
Python, RAG, Prompt Engineering, LangChain, Fine-tuning, FAISS, Vector Search, LLM-as-judge Evaluation, FastAPI

Built and shipped a production RAG-based customer-support assistant handling tier-1 tickets | Owns an offline LLM evaluation pipeline (LLM-as-judge) gating prompt changes | Led a 2-engineer feature squad
Technical Interview (Round 2) / In Progress (round 2)
Cleared recruiter screen and R1. Strong RAG/evaluation background; applying up-level from Senior to Lead.
Multi-agent orchestration (LangGraph)
Production GenAI at scale
LLM evaluation pipelines
Technical leadership

Hi Meera Krishnan,

Thank you for your time. We're completing our review and will be in touch with next steps soon.

Best regards,
Northwind AI
